checks.a_analysis: The output text has been cross-referenced with the source PDF. All paragraphs and headings in the Executive Summary, cover page, and Appendices match the source material. No fabricated text or sections were found. The placeholder for omitted sections is considered a structural marker for the evaluation context rather than fabrication.

Every phone number, address, and organizational title in Appendix A was verified against pages 31-34 of the source PDF. All data points, including secondary phone numbers (cell) and designee contact information, were found to be 100% accurate. URLs found in the text match the source document exactly.

The output contains the cover page, blank page, executive summary, and the complete set of appendices. While the middle sections of the report are omitted, this is explicitly noted as a technical constraint of the sample rather than an error in conversion. The sections that are included are complete and consistent with the source PDF.

Tables in Appendix A and Appendix C were audited. Appendix A tables on pages 31, 32, 33, and 34 are fully represented with all members listed. Appendix C tables across pages 36, 37, and 38 are also fully represented in the JSON output, preserving the extensive list of titles and custodians without row loss.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Within the provided sections (Cover, Executive Summary, Appendices), the text is preserved accurately. No paragraphs or significant sentences were omitted from these sections.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording in the output is highly faithful to the source. Differences in line breaks or heading normalization (such as splitting the multi-line title on the cover page) are considered acceptable reformatting for structured data and do not alter the informational content.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the provided JSON and the source document across all pages (including cover, executive summary, and appendices). I found no instances of duplicated text blocks, repeated sections, or content appearing twice in the output that only appears once in the source. The reading order and element flow follow the source document without repeating content.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. The main title on the cover page uses H1, and major sections such as the 'Executive Summary' and the Appendices use H2. Subsections within the Executive Summary (e.g., 'NC OneMap:', 'Orthoimagery:') are correctly tagged as H3. No levels are skipped, and the nesting accurately reflects the structural organization of the source.

The table structure in Appendix C is corrupted across page breaks. In the source document, the table 'FY 2013-14: Data and Web Service Updates and New Releases' spans pages 36, 37, and 38. While page 36 contains the correct headers ('TITLE', 'CUSTODIAN'), page 37 and page 38 do not repeat these headers. However, the output JSON for Section 37 (Page 37) and Section 38 (Page 38) incorrectly identifies the first row of data on those pages as header rows. This misidentifies data content (e.g., 'NCDOT Pavement Condition Survey, Continuously Reinforced Concrete' and 'Shellfish Growing Areas') as structural column headers.
checks.b_evidence_source: The first entry on Page 37 is 'NCDOT Pavement Condition Survey, Continuously Reinforced Concrete' (TITLE) and 'NCDOT-GIS' (CUSTODIAN). The first entry on Page 38 is 'Shellfish Growing Areas' (TITLE) and 'DENR, NC Shellfish Sanitation and Recreational Water Quality Section' (CUSTODIAN). These are data rows.
checks.b_evidence_output: "is_header_row": true for the first row of tables in Section 37 and Section 38.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the output faithfully follows the source document. It proceeds from the cover page to the executive summary and through to the appendices in a logical, top-to-bottom, page-by-page manner that matches the original intended flow of information.

Lists, particularly those in the Executive Summary, have been converted correctly. The source uses checkmark symbols which have been appropriately represented as unordered lists in the JSON. The grouping of list items matches the source text.

URLs present in the text (such as http://data.nconemap.com and ncleg.net links) are preserved as text within the paragraph content. While they are not specifically wrapped in interactive link objects, the text content is accurate and complete, and no fabricated links were injected.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output effectively preserves the visual organization and flow of information from the source PDF. The multi-page PDF structure is converted into a standard responsive single-column HTML layout. Sidebars, such as the 'NC OneMap' block on PDF page 6, are integrated logically into the visual flow. Section headers, bulleted lists, and the overall sequence of content match the source document's intent.

The tables in Appendix A (Council Members) and Appendix C (Data Updates) are correctly rendered as HTML tables. They feature distinct headers (e.g., '2014 Member', 'Title and Organization', 'Appointing Authority' in Appendix A; 'TITLE', 'CUSTODIAN' in Appendix C) and well-aligned data. The visual presentation allows a sighted user to easily distinguish between rows and columns and read the content clearly.

All meaningful images from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the NCGICC logo (page 1), diagrams like Figure 1 (page 7) and Figure 2 (page 8), various geographic maps (Figures 4 and 7), illustrative photos (pages 9, 12, 14, 16, 17), and the NCGIS2015 banner (page 24). The visuals are correctly placed relative to their surrounding text.

There is no evidence of duplicated body content within the HTML output. Paragraphs, tables, and images appear exactly once in the document body, matching their single appearance in the source PDF (excluding headings repeated for navigation/TOC purposes, which is expected behavior).
